matches.moment: Diallo's direct run splits the Curacoa right side in the 38th; Adingra punishes a tired defensive shape late.
Germany scored 3+ in 8 of their last 9 internationals; Ecuador have 0 goals across 2 games with their creator suspended.
Sweden's must-win desperation forces them to push 5 men forward from the 60th minute -- Japan's transitions become lethal.
Tunisia conceded 5 to Sweden and 4 to Japan. Netherlands' GD race means Koeman keeps pushing; Summerville adds the 4th in the 82nd.
USA scored 4 and 2 in their first two games -- even rotating they finish. Guler gets his tournament goal but too late.
A Paraguay corner creates chaos in the six-yard box; Gomez pounces on the second ball after Souttar's clearance falls short.

verdict: This tournament is averaging 3.1 goals per game -- the highest group-stage rate in decades -- and today's six-match final slate will not be an exception. Germany have scored 3+ in eight of their last nine internationals against an Ecuador side with zero goals in two games. The Netherlands face a Tunisia defence that has leaked nine goals in two games, with a GD race providing motivation to keep pushing -- 4-0 is where the data points. Japan's counter-attacking system becomes a scoring machine when Sweden must commit forwards in the 60th minute. The single genuinely tight match is Paraguay vs Australia, where Almiron's suspension and Australia's structural incentive to park the bus create the one legitimate case for a 1-0. Everywhere else today, the goals are coming. -- The Analyst.

players.name: Ayase Ueda
players.watch: Exploit Sweden's high defensive line on Japan's third-man runs -- scores in the 18th before Sweden can establish their shape

players.name: Arda Guler
players.stat: 0 tournament goals, est. 1.3 xG/game created -- most creative on an eliminated side
players.watch: The tournament goal that was coming -- curling finish from 20m; arrives too late to change the result

players.name: Cody Gakpo
players.stat: 1 tournament goal; NED averaging 1.70 xG/game; market over/under vs. TUN set at 3.5
players.watch: Opens the scoring in the 12th -- Netherlands start fast to bank GD before Japan can set a high target

players.name: Deniz Undav
players.stat: 5 sub goal contributions this tournament; starts today
players.watch: First full 90 vs. Ecuador -- scores early to end any lingering ECU hope

players.name: Viktor Gyokeres
players.stat: 7 qualifying goals (0 WC tournament goals yet)
players.watch: Scores through Isak to make it 1-1 at 38' but Sweden's defensive exposure on the counter undoes the fight

players.name: Simon Adingra
players.stat: Regular PL starter for Brighton 2025/26 -- pace and direct running on the left
players.watch: Punishes Curacoa's tired legs in the 74th minute -- Room made 15 saves vs. Ecuador; this time the volume breaks through
